skill.name: youtube-transcript-analysis-api-skill
skill.description: Extracts YouTube transcripts through BrowserAct workflows and analyzes video messaging, audience intent, pain points, CTAs, and content gaps.
skill.summary: Extract and analyze YouTube transcripts for competitive content research.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The static critical combination is not confirmed as malicious. The Python script makes expected BrowserAct API calls using a user-supplied bearer token, while SKILL.md command and weak-crypto alerts are documentation false positives. Publication is reasonable with a warning that video inputs and the BrowserAct API key are sent to a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Third-party API access with bearer token
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it in Authorization headers to the BrowserAct API while submitting YouTube URLs or search keywords. This matches the skill purpose, but users should know their API key and requested video data leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly reads an API key and uses it as a bearer token for a hardcoded BrowserAct endpoint. The behavior is expected for this skill, so the risk is disclosure to a third-party service rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Documented shell commands are not hidden execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md contains example commands and output templates that instruct an agent how to invoke the bundled Python script. I did not find evidence of shell metacharacter injection, dynamic command construction, or background execution in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation and fenced example output. It does not contain executable loader code or an instruction to run arbitrary user-controlled shell fragments.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography alerts are markdown text false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak-cryptography detections point at prose, headings, and parameter examples in SKILL.md. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic verification code is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe video analysis workflow details, not cryptographic algorithms. The Python script imports no crypto library and performs no crypto operations.

low_findings.title: Status and error checks are operational, not reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The scanner flagged checks for API response strings, task IDs, and task statuses as reconnaissance. These checks are normal polling and error handling for the BrowserAct workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code inspects API responses from its own submitted task and prints user-facing errors. I did not find host, network, account, or filesystem enumeration beyond the BrowserAct task workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network plus credential access heuristic requires disclosure
dangerous_patterns.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The static critical heuristic combines network requests, environment credential access, and documented command invocation. The reviewed context supports a legitimate BrowserAct client, but the marketplace listing should disclose third-party API usage and API-key handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combination exists in code and documentation, but the endpoint and credential are tied to the stated BrowserAct service. There is no evidence of unrelated exfiltration or obfuscated control flow.

content.value_statement: Manual video review is slow when teams need competitor messaging and audience insights. This skill extracts transcripts through BrowserAct and turns them into concise strategic analysis.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python script for single-video transcript extraction by YouTube URL.
Runs batch transcript extraction from YouTube search keywords, upload date, and result limit.
Uses BrowserAct workflow templates and polls until extraction finishes or times out.
Returns video metadata, channel details, engagement fields, and transcript text.
Guides the agent to produce an eight-dimension content strategy analysis.
Handles invalid keys, concurrency limits, failed tasks, and one retry path.
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key in BROWSERACT_API_KEY.
Sends video URLs, search terms, and the API key to BrowserAct.
Depends on BrowserAct workflow availability and YouTube transcript access.
Does not verify transcript accuracy beyond noting short or noisy transcripts.

use_cases.title: Benchmark Competitor Video Strategy
use_cases.description: Compare competitor videos to identify shared messages, CTAs, audience assumptions, and content gaps.

use_cases.title: Extract Customer Pain Points
use_cases.description: Analyze transcripts for repeated problems, emotional language, and unresolved viewer questions.

use_cases.title: Research Trending Topics
use_cases.description: Batch analyze recent videos in a niche to summarize common claims, hooks, and whitespace.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this YouTube video transcript for messaging, audience, pain points, CTA, and content gaps: [YouTube URL]

prompt_templates.title: Compare Recent Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube for [keyword], analyze [number] videos from [upload date], and compare common themes and gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Audience Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these YouTube videos with extra focus on target viewer profile, intent, pain points, and language level: [URLs or keyword]

prompt_templates.title: Build Competitive Messaging Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze competitor videos for hooks, evidence, positioning, conversion strategy, and missed topics. Produce a concise messaging brief with opportunities.

output_examples.output: The result includes video metadata, a concise hook analysis, the core thesis, audience profile, pain points, CTA strategy, credibility signals, and three strategic takeaways.

output_examples.input: Compare three recent videos for a keyword.
output: Lists each video's publisher, title, and URL.
Summarizes each video across the eight analysis dimensions.
Compares common value propositions, recurring pain points, and industry whitespace.

output_examples.input: Find content gaps in competitor videos.
output_examples.output: The response separates creator weaknesses, unanswered viewer questions, and broader topics competitors did not cover.

best_practices: Confirm the BrowserAct API key is available before running extraction.
Use a small batch limit first to control API cost and review quality.
Ask for a clear analysis focus when the user needs strategic depth.
anti_patterns: Do not publish full raw transcripts when a concise analysis is enough.
Do not run large batch jobs without confirming scope and cost expectations.
Do not treat extracted transcripts as perfect when they are short or auto-generated.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY so the script can call BrowserAct workflows.

faq.question: Can it analyze a single YouTube video?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a YouTube URL and the skill runs the single-video workflow.

faq.question: Can it analyze several videos at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide keywords, an upload date filter, and a result limit for batch mode.

faq.question: What data does it send outside my machine?
faq.answer: It sends the BrowserAct API key plus the video URL or search parameters to BrowserAct.

faq.question: Does it store transcripts locally?
faq.answer: No persistent local storage is shown in the reviewed code. Results are printed to standard output.

faq.question: What analysis does it produce?
faq.answer: It covers hooks, core messaging, audience intent, pain points, viral drivers, evidence, CTAs, and content gaps.
